author: allexcd
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Read-only, exclusion-based local bridge for using vault notes through MCP clients.

manifest.author: allexcd
manifest.description: Read-only, exclusion-based local bridge for using vault notes through MCP clients.
manifest.name: MCP Vault Bridge

name: MCP Vault Bridge

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Fixes

- Addressed the remaining ObsidianReviewBot findings from the community plugin review.
- Cleaned up async test mocks and bridge helpers without changing runtime behavior.
- Adjusted Obsidian file/metadata typings used by the local bridge.
- Reworded a few settings messages to satisfy Obsidian UI text checks.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Highlights

- Added smarter vault-wide question handling with the new `ask_vault` MCP tool.
- Improved embedding usage so conceptual questions can use semantic search automatically when embeddings are enabled.
- Added clearer MCP client config examples, including an embeddings-enabled setup for LM Studio or other OpenAI-compatible embedding servers.
- Improved first-run behavior for Community Plugin installs: the plugin now installs as the standard three Obsidian files and materializes its local MCP runtime files after enablement.
- Added ObsidianReviewBot-focused cleanup for Community Plugin review readiness.
- Improved docs around indexing, embeddings, refresh behavior, and prompt examples.

### Notes

- Embeddings remain optional. Without them, the plugin still uses SQLite full-text search.
- With embeddings enabled, vectors are stored locally in the plugin’s SQLite index.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Fixes Obsidian Community Plugin installs by making the standard three installed files self-contained.
- Automatically creates the local `mcp-server.cjs` and runtime `package.json` after the plugin is enabled.
- Keeps `node_modules` out of the plugin bundle; SQLite dependencies are still installed only from plugin settings.
- Aligns the zip/build output with what Obsidian actually installs.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Updates the plugin metadata for Obsidian Community Plugins compatibility.
- Renames the public plugin identity to **MCP Vault Bridge**.
- Includes required release assets for community plugin installation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## MCP Vault Bridge 0.3.0

### Highlights

- Renamed the plugin from **Obsidian MCP** to **MCP Vault Bridge** for community plugin submission.
- Changed the plugin id to `mcp-vault-bridge`, which follows Obsidian community plugin requirements.
- Updated build, install, package, and release assets to use the `mcp-vault-bridge` plugin folder and zip name.
- Kept the same read-only MCP bridge behavior introduced in `0.2.0`.

### Notes

- If you installed a previous manual build under `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-mcp`, install this release under `.obsidian/plugins/mcp-vault-bridge`.
- After switching folders, copy the token again from plugin settings and update LM Studio or Claude Desktop configs.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Highlights

- Added automatic first-run indexing so notes can appear without manually running `refresh_index`.
- Improved setup reliability with clearer bridge, runtime, and token status messages.
- Made bridge tokens safer by generating them per plugin install instead of reusing one global token.
- Fixed token copy/regenerate issues when Obsidian SecretStorage is unavailable or rejects a key.
- Improved bridge startup errors, including clearer messages for port conflicts.
- Fixed test/build reliability for the monorepo.

### Notes

- Embeddings are still off by default and must be enabled explicitly.
- Vault exclusions are still respected during automatic indexing.
- The plugin bundle still ships without `node_modules`; install runtime dependencies from settings.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What is this?

**Obsidian MCP** is a read-only bridge that connects your Obsidian vault to MCP-capable AI hosts — Claude Desktop and LM Studio. Once installed, your AI assistant can search, read, and navigate your notes without you copying and pasting anything.

## Features

- **Full-text search** across all included notes via a local SQLite index
- **Semantic search** with optional local embeddings (LM Studio + `nomic-embed-text-v1.5` or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
- **Read notes, metadata, links, and backlinks** by path
- **Exclusion-based privacy** — exclude folders, files, or tags before connecting any MCP host; hidden folders, `.obsidian`, `.trash`, and `.git` are always blocked
- **Token-gated bridge** — a bearer token generated by the plugin controls all access; stored in Obsidian SecretStorage when available
- **No write tools** — the plugin exposes no write, delete, shell, or command-execution capabilities
- **No telemetry**

## MCP tools

`vault_status` · `refresh_index` · `index_status` · `list_notes` · `search_vault` · `read_note` · `get_note_metadata` · `get_note_links` · `related_notes`

## Install

1. Download `obsidian-mcp-0.1.0.zip` below, unzip it, and copy the `obsidian-mcp` folder into your vault.

## Notes
- This is a developer preview. The plugin archive does not bundle `node_modules` — the `SQLite` runtime is downloaded on first use via the plugin settings.
- Keep Obsidian open while the MCP server is in use.
- Review your exclusions before connecting an MCP host. Vault content returned by tools may be forwarded to a cloud model by the host.
